tool.name: Taffy index
tool.description: creates an index file of a TAF or MAF file
tool.command:
## Set variables @SET_UNCOMPRESS_NAMES@
## Uncompress input files if necessary and create symlinks for uncompressed files @UNCOMPRESS_TAF_AND_MAF@
## Run main command taffy index --logLevel 'INFO' --blockSize $blockSize --inputFile '$uncompressed_input'

param.label: Block size
param.help: Write an index line for a specified interval of this many bases

tool.help: Taffy index creates a .tai index file for a given TAF or MAF file, enabling efficient random access to specific regions of the alignment. The index is created by scanning the alignment file and recording the file offset and genomic position for the reference sequence whenever a new contig begins or a specified interval of bases has passed. Therefore, the index file is strictly specific to the exact TAF or MAF file it was generated from. The index is stored as: <SEQUENCE_NAME> <POSITION> <FILE_OFFSET>, where subsequent entries for the same sequence use delta encoding (stored as * <RELATIVE_POS> <RELATIVE_OFFSET>).
